extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won a reduced sprint after AKOV Niels shut down late attacks, outsprinting MEZGEC Luka in final kilometer
tactical_patterns: AKOV Niels performed dual role closing late SENECHAL attack then celebrating teammate victory, unusual domestique strength
Multiple breakaway attempts collapsed on 13.6km local circuit lap, suggesting final circuit neutralizes early moves
SIMMONS Quinn made front split as junior world champion, revealing early-career positioning skill in reduced bunch

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: seventh Bretagne Classic, never won before, dominated sprint after 247km suggesting improved endurance in non-GT preparation
rider_signals.AKOV Niels: positive: strongest in race per commentary, closed SENECHAL attack before leadout, wasted on domestique duty
rider_signals.MEZGEC Luka: neutral: made front split but lost sprint by wide margin, positioning better than finishing speed
rider_signals.SENECHAL Florian: neutral: attacked but neutralized by AKOV, podium finish suggests tired sprint not lack of form
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: positive: made winning split in first World Tour year, junior champion credentials translating to senior positioning
